alerts.title: Netanyahu orders IDF strikes on Beirut's Dahieh; Hezbollah front expands
alerts.summary: Reuters, BBC, Al-Monitor, Times of Israel, ynetnews and Chinese state media report PM Netanyahu and DM Katz have ordered the IDF to strike targets in Beirut's southern suburbs (Dahieh), citing Hezbollah 'ceasefire violations.' The order expands beyond prior decapitation strikes to systematic targeting of Hezbollah's urban stronghold, raising risk of mass-casualty events and broader Iranian retaliation.

alerts.title: US intel: Iran restored entry to 18 bombed missile sites, 'exceeded all timelines'
alerts.summary: Times of Israel and The Media Line cite US intelligence assessments that Iran has reopened most entrances to 18 underground missile sites struck during the war, with reconstitution pace 'exceeding all timelines.' Combined with Jerusalem Post reporting that Iran is 'ready to fire missiles across Middle East after digging out bombed tunnels,' the development materially shortens warning time for a renewed Iranian salvo.

alerts.title: Kazakhstan offers to take custody of Iran's enriched uranium stockpile
alerts.summary: Financial Times, Times of Israel and Kurdistan24 report IAEA Director-General Rafael Grossi disclosed Kazakhstan's willingness to store Iran's HEU stockpile if a deal is reached. With Iranian enrichment at 60% U-235 and breakout estimated at 1–2 weeks, the Astana offer represents the first concrete off-ramp for the nuclear file amid active US-Iran kinetic exchanges.

alerts.title: Trump personally editing draft US-Iran deal on uranium and Strait of Hormuz
alerts.summary: CBS News reports a source close to the talks says President Trump has personally revised text of a possible US-Iran agreement covering enriched uranium limits and Strait of Hormuz access. The disclosure runs in parallel to fresh kinetic exchanges, suggesting a track-two diplomatic channel remains open despite the PBS-reported US accusation that Iran violated the ceasefire by striking Kuwait.

alerts.title: IDF names Staff-Sgt Adam Tzarfati killed in Hezbollah drone strike in south Lebanon
alerts.summary: Haaretz and Jerusalem Post report the IDF has named Staff-Sergeant Adam Tzarfati as killed by a Hezbollah drone explosion in southern Lebanon (~33.5°N, 35.7°E). The named KIA corroborates the prior ME Spectator (~46K views) report of a Hezbollah suicide drone at Beit Hillel and reinforces a sustained pattern of Hezbollah FPV/loitering munitions inflicting IDF casualties during the ground push.

analysis_note: Threat level remains at 9/10 with Brent at $93.7; Israel is widening the Lebanon front into Beirut's Dahieh as US intelligence concedes Iran's missile-site reconstitution has outpaced expectations, even as a Kazakhstan-mediated uranium off-ramp and Trump-edited deal text suggest a parallel diplomatic track.
